title: Sync topology configuration and scheduling
purpose: Deliver configurable sync topology (full-mesh, ring, custom pairs) and the sync scheduler that triggers periodic syncs based on the configured topology and interval. Any topology converges to consistency given sufficient sync rounds.
task: Implement sync topology configuration: full-mesh (every node syncs with every other), ring (each node syncs with its successor), and custom (explicit sync pairs). Implement the sync scheduler as a beamr process that triggers periodic syncs based on topology and interval. The scheduler fires pull-syncs to the target nodes according to the topology map. Does not implement the sync protocol (DIST-001) or merge logic (DIST-002).

requirements.title: Topology configuration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL accept a sync topology on database creation: FullMesh, Ring, or Custom(pairs). THE SYSTEM SHALL derive the sync partner list from the topology. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT have a default topology — the caller must choose explicitly.
acceptance: SyncTopology::FullMesh with 4 nodes produces 6 sync pairs (all combinations)
SyncTopology::Ring with 4 nodes produces 4 sync pairs (each to its successor)
SyncTopology::Custom accepts an explicit list of (source, target) pairs
Creating a distributed database without a topology is an error

requirements.title: Sync scheduler
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a sync scheduler as a supervised beamr process. THE SYSTEM SHALL trigger pull-syncs to partner nodes at the configured interval according to the topology. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT block the shard actor's write path while syncing.
acceptance: The scheduler fires sync operations at the configured interval
Sync targets are determined by the topology
The scheduler is supervised — a crash restarts it
Write operations are not blocked during scheduled syncs

requirements.title: Convergence guarantee
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure that any topology converges to consistency given sufficient sync rounds. THE SYSTEM SHALL document the convergence properties of each topology: FullMesh converges in 1 round, Ring converges in N-1 rounds, Custom depends on the pair graph connectivity.
acceptance: FullMesh topology converges all nodes to the same state in one sync round
Ring topology converges in at most N-1 rounds for N nodes
Custom topology converges if and only if the pair graph is connected

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the sync protocol or node transfer — that is DIST-001
SHALL NOT implement merge or consistency modes — that is DIST-002
SHALL NOT implement dynamic topology changes at runtime — topology is set at creation
SHALL NOT implement node discovery — uses beamr's existing distribution connection manager
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no errors
grep -r 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/sync/ --include='*.rs' -l finds no non-test files
No file in crates/haematite/src/sync/ exceeds 500 lines
